refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall synchronize worksite model state changes across the distributed system with a p95 latency ≤ 1.0s to ensure operational safety and productivity.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was a high-level goal; refinement adds specific performance targets (p95) and links the requirement to the safety-critical nature of the system.
refined_fr.verification_idea: End-to-end latency profiling using timestamped telemetry packets from sensor ingestion to UI rendering on the tele-operator console.
sub_frs: Capture worksite state changes via onboard sensors.
Transmit state updates over the network link.
Process and merge updates into the global worksite model.

candidate_dps: Edge-computed delta-compression with prioritized pub-sub messaging
selected_dp.selected_dp: Edge-computed delta-compression with prioritized pub-sub messaging

coupling_check.notes: Decoupling is achieved by sending only state changes (deltas) rather than full model snapshots.

assumptions_risks: Assumes stable network link availability for p95 targets.
Risk of out-of-order packet delivery affecting model consistency.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Incorporate a local 'dead-reckoning' buffer to handle transient network jitter.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The overarching system-of-systems container that orchestrates data flow between vehicles and safety services.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingestion of raw sensor and state data from the vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: The transport layer for distributing model updates to remote stakeholders.

module_containment_scores.notes: Primary owner of the global worksite model logic.
Only contains local state; cannot satisfy global model update requirements alone.
Consumer of the model, not the primary maintainer.
Requires the model for policy enforcement but does not manage the sync logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module provides the necessary centralized orchestration to manage multi-vehicle state fusion.
containment_risks: Logic diffusion if delta-compression is implemented inconsistently across different vehicle types.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define a standardized 'Worksite Update' API within the NGHE Solution to enforce uniform data structures.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The delta-compression DP directly addresses the 1.0s latency constraint by reducing payload size.

diffusion_risks: High risk of logic leaking into the 'Vehicle' module if sensor pre-processing is too heavy.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution is the correct boundary for the global model, but interface contracts with the Vehicle must be strict.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High; latency can be measured at the NGHE Solution boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation to the system-level solution module.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define the 'Network Link' performance requirements as a prerequisite for this IM to meet the FR.

decision.justification: The high-level FR and DP are validated, but the sub-functions (Capture, Transmit, Process) require their own DP selection and IM allocation at the next level of detail.
next_level_sub_frs: Capture: The Vehicle shall extract worksite deltas from raw sensor streams in < 100ms.
Transmit: The Network Link shall prioritize Worksite Update packets over general telemetry.
Process: The NGHE Solution shall merge deltas into the master model in < 200ms.

assumptions: Network Link supports Quality of Service (QoS) tagging.
open_risks: Impact of high-density worksites (many vehicles) on the 1.0s p95 target.
evidence_needed: Network simulation results for multi-node delta synchronization.
